summary: Iteration 2 testing pass. Backend: 9/9 new tests passed covering POST /api/cipher/suggest (meeting-intent true/false, too-few messages, non-member 403), video + audio message storage, and restaurant Unsplash realism. Regression: 24/25 on existing suite (only known test-side email-case assertion fails). Frontend: All 6 new iteration-2 capabilities verified — attach-video present / attach-audio removed, mic button (tap no crash), restaurant Unsplash image URL, Cipher meeting-suggestion banner appeared ~5s after 3 meeting msgs and Accept posted the calendar card, and tapping the group chat header routed to /group-info/{id} with member rows + admin chip + Leave button.

minor.issue: Pre-existing test-side email-casing assertion failure carried from iteration 1. Backend lowercases email (by design) but the assertion expects exact case. Not a backend bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Unsplash URL prefix is correct (starts with https://images.unsplash.com) so the testable contract passes, but in the visible chat the <Image> renders as a grey placeholder (photo does not resolve). Most likely the LLM occasionally returns a photo-id that 404s. Consider validating image_url with a HEAD probe on the backend, or maintaining a curated pool of guaranteed-resolving Unsplash ids per cuisine on the server side.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warnings still present: `shadow*` style props and `expo-av` (to be removed in SDK 54). Non-blocking but worth migrating to `boxShadow` and `expo-audio`/`expo-video`.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Backend /cipher/ask: harden restaurant image_url realism — either HEAD-check the returned Unsplash URL and fall back to the curated default, or seed the LLM with a cuisine→photo-id map so every card has a guaranteed-resolving image.
(LOW) Migrate `shadow*` → `boxShadow` and `expo-av` → `expo-audio`/`expo-video` to silence SDK 55 deprecation warnings.
(LOW) Optional: assert return shape of /api/cipher/suggest in suggestion-banner code — current UX handles it already, but tightening prevents regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py cipher_suggest uses datetime.now().timestamp() in session_id — fine, but each call spawns a fresh LlmChat; acceptable.
cipher_suggest correctly filters out @cipher lines and requires >=2 real messages before calling the LLM — good cost guard.
Video/audio stored as full base64 in Mongo (acknowledged as MOCKED in the request). For any real traffic this will bloat the DB — replace with real S3/CloudStorage before prod.
server.py is now ~820 lines; should be split into routers (auth/messages/cipher/users/2fa) to stay under the 700-line guideline.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of iteration-2 flows verified; 1 MEDIUM realism issue on restaurant image

seed_data_creation: Tests only create transient TEST_* conversations/users which are cleaned up in-test. One group 'TEST_GroupIter2' was created during Playwright run and left behind for Alice; safe to delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-2 tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration2_backend.py (pytest) and pass in ~21s. Frontend viewport 412x880. The suggestion banner actually polls more aggressively than 30s in practice — banner appeared within ~5s. To create a group via Playwright, pick members by iterating query_selector_all('[data-testid^="group-member-"]') and clicking rows whose inner_text contains the target name (direct .get_by_text click fails because the clickable is the parent row). Direct navigation to /group-create still works after login (iteration 1 HIGH bug appears resolved — searchUsers returned users successfully this run). MOCKED: video/audio stored as base64 in Mongo (no real S3), no WebSockets (polling only).
rca_of_issue: MEDIUM: Restaurant card Unsplash image renders as grey placeholder. Repro: login alice → open Bob DM → send '@Cipher find an italian restaurant in Connaught Place' → card appears with correct name/rating/price/Book Now, but the <Image source={{uri:image_url}}/> stays grey. Root cause: LLM sometimes hallucinates photo-ids on images.unsplash.com that 404 (e.g. photo-ids that don't exist). Because the URL *prefix* is correct, the backend contract passes, but the visible UX is degraded. Mitigation: (a) backend validates image_url via HEAD and substitutes the curated fallback id when non-200, or (b) CIPHER_SYSTEM prompt is updated with an explicit list of ~20 verified photo ids per cuisine so the LLM can only pick from known-good urls.
